items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Dunvegan Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* highlands
*County:* Isle of Skye
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 13th century
*Coords:* 57.448, -6.59
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dunvegan_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Dunvegan%20Castle%20Isle%20of%20Skye%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/74/Highland_-_Dunvegan_Castle_-_20240524140144.jpg/330px-Highland_-_Dunvegan_Castle_-_20240524140144.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* https://www.dunvegancastle.com/ add source 

*Description:*
Dunvegan Castle is located 1 mile (1.6 km) to the north of Dunvegan on the Isle of Skye, off the west coast of Scotland. It is the seat of the MacLeod of MacLeod, chief of the Clan MacLeod. Probably a fortified site from the earliest times, the castle was first built in the 13th century and developed piecemeal over the centuries. In the 19th century the whole castle was remodelled in a mock-medieval style. The castle is built on an elevated rock overlooking an inlet on the eastern shore of Loch Dunvegan, a sea loch.

_Listing 1 of 10_

listing.desc: Dunvegan Castle is located 1 mile (1.6 km) to the north of Dunvegan on the Isle of Skye, off the west coast of Scotland. It is the seat of the MacLeod of MacLeod, chief of the Clan MacLeod. Probably a fortified site from the earliest times, the castle was first built in the 13th century and developed piecemeal over the centuries. In the 19th century the whole castle was remodelled in a mock-medieval style. The castle is built on an elevated rock overlooking an inlet on the eastern shore of Loch Dunvegan, a sea loch.

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[🔍 PENDING\]

*Name:* Mingary Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* highlands
*County:* Highland
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 13th century
*Coords:* 56.69361111, -6.08083333
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mingary_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Mingary%20Castle%20Highland%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/mingary-castle-3.jpg
*Gallery:* 8 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Flag Reasons:* needs-review
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://mingarycastle.co.uk/a

*Description:*
Mingary Castle, also known as Mingarry Castle, is a castle situated one mile southeast of the small village of Kilchoan in Lochaber, Scotland. Nestled on ridge of rock overlooking the sea, it was considered a strategically important site in terms of communication with overseas areas and as an entranceway to the Sound of Mull. Mingary is roughly hexagonal in shape with nine-foot-thick walls, thicker on the seaward side. The remains of the castle are protected as a category A listed building.

_Listing 2 of 10_

listing.desc: Mingary Castle, also known as Mingarry Castle, is a castle situated one mile southeast of the small village of Kilchoan in Lochaber, Scotland. Nestled on ridge of rock overlooking the sea, it was considered a strategically important site in terms of communication with overseas areas and as an entranceway to the Sound of Mull. Mingary is roughly hexagonal in shape with nine-foot-thick walls, thicker on the seaward side. The remains of the castle are protected as a category A listed building.

*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZN1-RJUt0HK5xAJZkExRs7n1Q0R-tik3JsR2kbfcyXlFtpU4ibyBmFSIi4lg0V8ek0L5qJw5o_fYvnFI3Qd0iJ2FICA4Az3HP6lrNWlX72_Qqr9vZYD-vh_0DDs1sLnGEastKgMeB_oUZr_Kg=s4800-w1200

*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* update google places link https://maps.app.goo.gl/vgiMLtPrFgYoSyVU6

*Description:*
Duntulm Castle stands ruined on the north coast of Trotternish, on the Isle of Skye in Scotland, near the hamlet of Duntulm. During the 17th century it was the seat of the chiefs of Clan MacDonald of Sleat. It is a scheduled monument.

_Listing 4 of 10_
